lede: A look at February 16 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Yuki Naito and Andrea Ranocchia, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Kherrata
events.desc: Five thousand people gathered in the town of Kherrata, Bejaia Province to mark the second anniversary of the Hirak protest movement. Demonstrations had been suspended because of the COVID-19 pandemic in Algeria.

events.title: February 2013 Quetta bombing
events.desc: A bomb blast at a market in Hazara Town, Quetta, Pakistan kills more than 80 people and injures 190 others.

events.title: Ash Wednesday bushfires
events.desc: The Ash Wednesday bushfires in Victoria and South Australia kill 75.

events.title: Haleyville, Alabama
events.desc: In Haleyville, Alabama, the first 9-1-1 emergency telephone system goes into service.

events.title: Fidel Castro
events.desc: Fidel Castro becomes Premier of Cuba after dictator Fulgencio Batista was overthrown on January 1.

events.title: Austrian Civil War
events.desc: The Austrian Civil War ends with the defeat of the Social Democrats and the Republikanischer Schutzbund.

events.title: Council of Lithuania
events.desc: The Council of Lithuania unanimously adopts the Act of Independence, declaring Lithuania an independent state.

events.title: Southern Cross Expedition
events.desc: The Southern Cross expedition led by Carsten Borchgrevink achieves a new Farthest South of 78° 50'S, making the first landing at the Great Ice Barrier.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: General Ulysses S. Grant captures Fort Donelson, Tennessee.

events.title: First Barbary War
events.desc: First Barbary War: Stephen Decatur leads a raid to burn the pirate-held frigate USS Philadelphia.

deaths.text: Jânio Quadros, Brazilian politician, 22nd President of Brazil

observances.text: Christian feast day: Juliana of Nicomedia (Catholic Church)
Christian feast day: Charles Todd Quintard (Episcopal Church (USA))
Day of the Shining Star (Kim Jong Il's birthday) (North Korea)
Restoration of Lithuania's Statehood Day, celebrate the independence of Lithuania from Russia and Germany in 1918 (Lithuania)
